name: Conversations
description: Unified record of every meaningful interaction with a contact — emails, calls, meetings, LinkedIn messages, SMS. Replaces channel-specific collections so scripts read one shape regardless of channel.

properties.description: Stable unique identifier — typically <provider>:<source_id> for idempotent re-delivery.
Channel/medium of the conversation.
Who initiated the conversation.
Subject line for emails; topic for calls; title for meetings.
Full text content — email body, call transcript, meeting notes, message body. Kept as-is for re-extraction.
First 200 characters of body, for quick scanning in lists.
Primary contact email this conversation is associated with.
Company domain when no specific contact is identified.
Email address or identifier of the sender.
List of recipient identifiers (emails for email, phone numbers for SMS, etc.).
When the conversation occurred in the source system.
Length in seconds for calls and meetings.
Engagement state.
AI-inferred sentiment of the conversation.
AI-generated one-paragraph summary of what was discussed and decided.
Concrete next steps extracted from the conversation. Each entry: { owner, action, due_date }.
Campaign or sequence this conversation belongs to, when applicable.
Step number within the active sequence (1, 2, 3...). Null for one-off conversations.
CRM where this conversation originated.
Native CRM record ID (Email, Call, Meeting object) for writeback.
Names of scripts/operations that have already consumed this conversation. Prevents duplicate processing.
